content:
One thing that saves a lot of cleanup later: crawl the staging site and compare indexable pages before launch, not after.
I usually check four things:

• Canonicals point to the final production URLs, not staging URLs or the homepage.

• Important templates do not accidentally ship with noindex from the staging environment.

• Internal links use clean final paths instead of mixed absolute staging links, redirect chains, or query-param versions.

• Server-rendered metadata is present in the initial HTML for key pages, especially titles, descriptions, canonicals, hreflang, and structured data if used.
The useful part is not just catching broken tags. It catches template-level mistakes. If one product/category/service template is wrong, hundreds of URLs can inherit the same SEO problem on launch day.
A 10-minute crawl diff before deploy is usually cheaper than discovering a week later that Google indexed the wrong canonical or ignored half the site.

  submitted by   /u/VRTCLS
[link]   [comments]
